identity.title: Pottery, Ceramics, and Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing

lens.included_activities: Independent US lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly supply external commercial, construction, industrial, utility, or distribution customers with ceramic plumbing fixtures, electrical ceramic supplies and insulators, magnets, chemical stoneware, or repeat-production ceramic ware.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive plants, non-control vehicles, artist studios, bespoke decorative pottery, direct-to-consumer craft businesses, and firms without recurring or repeat outsourced customer relationships.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat purchase orders, distributor programs, project and specification sales, and approved-supplier relationships for formed, glazed, and fired ceramic products, usually billed per unit, lot, or project release.
lens.deviation_from_default: The NAICS code spans studio-like pottery and repeat industrial or construction manufacturing with incompatible customer economics. It is narrowed to recurring external-customer manufacturing rather than one-off artistic or craft production.

a.rationale: The BLS subsector profile highlights molders, shapers, machine operators, material movers, production supervisors, and drivers, indicating a physical workforce. AI exposure is concentrated in quoting, product and glaze documentation, production planning, purchasing, inspection assistance, maintenance triage, order handling, and administration rather than shaping, glazing, kiln operation, casting, handling, and finishing.
a.change_evidence: Payroll-weighted task inventories from 10-15 eligible ceramic and fixture plants, including current software, machine automation, and time by commercial, technical, production, quality, and administrative task, would narrow the range.
caveats: BLS occupation evidence covers all NAICS 327 rather than 327110 or the narrowed lens.
Exposure may differ materially between plumbing fixtures, technical ceramics, magnets, and tableware.

rho.rationale: Assisted quoting, scheduling, documentation, defect classification, and knowledge retrieval are feasible within five years. Legacy kilns and plant systems, batch variability, sparse quality data, safety, customer qualification, product aesthetics, and required physical intervention reduce implemented share.
rho.change_evidence: Multi-plant pre/post results for AI-supported inspection, kiln or process analytics, scheduling, quoting, maintenance, and order administration, with sustained labor-hour impact, would materially change the estimate.
caveats: No 327110-specific AI implementation cohort was found.
Conventional automation or capital replacement is excluded unless AI is the enabling constraint.

e.rationale: Many commercial, industrial, and construction ceramic producers can have repeat external-customer programs, but the code also contains artistic, consumer, captive, project-only, or founder-dependent businesses outside the lens. Eligibility further declines with poor recurrence, excessive customer concentration, or nontransferable craft knowledge.
e.change_evidence: A firm-by-firm classification of the supplied 80 candidates by product, customer channel, repeat revenue, independence, certifications, customer concentration, and founder dependence would directly measure eligibility.
caveats: The supplied firm count is an ESTIMATE based on size classes and an assumed margin.
The establishment-based industry definition does not reveal firm revenue model or transferability.

s5.rationale: A minority of eligible private manufacturers may change control in five years, while family ownership, long holding periods, plant-specific capital, environmental and kiln liabilities, weak recent performance, and strategic ownership can inhibit transactions. Minority recapitalizations and internal reorganizations are excluded.
s5.change_evidence: A denominator-matched five-year control-sale census from PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, Axial, specialist brokers, and verified ownership histories for eligible 327110 firms would update the probability.
caveats: No directly observed eligible-firm control-transfer series was found.
Small private deals and failed processes are underreported.

q.rationale: Per-unit, lot, and project pricing can retain internal savings, particularly for qualified technical ceramics or differentiated design. Plumbing fixtures and repeat ware face distributor bargaining, project bids, import competition, commodity alternatives, and periodic price resets that share benefits with customers.
q.change_evidence: Contract and invoice analysis across technical ceramic, fixture, electrical, and repeat-ware customers measuring bid frequency, price resets, open-book provisions, rebates, and margin persistence after productivity projects would materially change the range.
caveats: The Census definition supports product segmentation but not billing terms.
Value capture is likely much higher in qualified technical niches than in imported or distributor-compared fixtures and ware.

d5.rationale: Replacement, renovation, utilities, construction, and industrial uses support continuing demand, but housing and nonresidential cycles, imports, customer destocking, product substitution, and domestic capacity rationalization create material downside. The broader NAICS 327 subsector's real output fell 4.5% in 2023, 9.1% in 2024, and 0.7% in 2025 after rising 2.3% in 2022.
d5.change_evidence: Constant-price shipment forecasts by plumbing, electrical ceramic, technical ceramic, magnet, and repeat-ware segment, linked to construction, renovation, utility, and industrial end-market forecasts and import penetration, would materially update the range.
caveats: BLS output data cover broader NAICS 327 and are historical annual changes, not a five-year forecast.
The lens spans products with very different construction, consumer, and industrial cycles.

o.rationale: The year-five basket consists of physical fired products requiring forming, glazing or materials processing, kiln control, inspection, traceability, and delivery by an accountable producer. Software can improve these activities but cannot supply the ceramic object; limited quantity may disappear through customer insourcing, alternate materials, redesign, or imports supplied without a domestic lens operator.
o.change_evidence: Customer engineering and procurement interviews on material substitution, offshoring, insourcing, modular construction, and qualification requirements across the included product segments would test the displacement allowance.
caveats: Imported products can displace a domestic operator even though a producer remains necessary somewhere.
Continued operator requirement does not imply unchanged domestic staffing or manufacturing process.

narrative.executive_view: The coherent opportunity lies in repeat commercial, construction, and industrial ceramics rather than one-off craft pottery. These are physical, often specification-sensitive products, but AI reaches only the information-heavy edge of a labor- and capital-intensive manufacturing process.
narrative.ai_impact: AI can assist quoting, specification retrieval, planning, defect classification, corrective actions, maintenance, order processing, and purchasing. Human operators remain necessary for forming, casting, glazing, kiln operation, material handling, finishing, inspection accountability, and production exceptions.
narrative.value_capture: Qualified technical products can retain savings, while plumbing fixtures and repeat ware face distributor pressure, project bids, imports, and periodic price resets. Contract mix matters more than nominal product category.
narrative.firm_availability: The supplied dataset estimates 80 firms in the EBITDA band, but code heterogeneity and craft or founder dependence reduce eligible share. Private control-transfer evidence is not directly matched to this population.
narrative.demand_durability: Renovation, replacement, utilities, construction, and industrial needs preserve a physical product basket, but recent broader-subsector output weakness, imports, and construction cyclicality warrant a wide downside range.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Product heterogeneity, domestic demand weakness, energy-intensive kilns, environmental liabilities, imports, and founder-dependent craft knowledge are major risks. Six-digit task exposure, implementation results, contracts, and transfers need direct diligence.
narrative.principal_driver: Specification-sensitive physical products and replacement or project demand preserve an accountable manufacturing role.
narrative.principal_weakness: A production-heavy labor mix and recent broader-subsector output contraction limit confidence in near-term AI labor leverage and demand.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 327110 Pottery, Ceramics, and Plumbing Fixture Manufacturing
sources.supports: Defines shaping, molding, glazing, and firing pottery, ceramics, plumbing fixtures, and electrical supplies; examples include vitreous china fixtures, ceramic or ferrite magnets, chemical stoneware, statuary, tableware, and porcelain electrical insulators.

sources.title: Industries at a Glance: Nonmetallic Mineral Product Manufacturing: NAICS 327
sources.supports: Lists major 2025 occupations including molders and shapers, forming-machine operators, material movers, production supervisors, and truck drivers; reports real-output changes of 2.3%, -4.5%, -9.1%, and -0.7% for 2022-2025.
